ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, high quality, clearly readable. Contains both ORIGINAL (page 1) and COPY (page 2) versions of the same certificate. Page 3 is an embedded copy of the commercial invoice within the certificate file.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF, high quality, clearly readable. Single page commercial invoice issued by Esswell International AB.

document_pairing.method: Certificate file (Document A) pages 1-2 contain ORIGINAL and COPY certificate. Page 3 of Document A is an embedded invoice copy (supplementary material per rule 8.5.0). Document B (Invoice file) page 1 is the independent commercial invoice used for verification. Pairing: Certificate from Document A page 1 (ORIGINAL) vs Invoice from Document B page 1.
document_pairing.notes: Page 3 of Document A is an embedded invoice copy identical to Document B page 1. Per rule 8.5.0, verification is performed against the SEPARATE invoice file (Document B), not the embedded copy in the certificate file.

criteria_met: Consignor 'Esswell International AB' identified in invoice header/footer
Consignee 'Burayu Development PLC' identified in invoice Customer/Applicant field
Customer Ref. 511202 in invoice matches proforma invoice reference in certificate

lc_validation.conclusion: The certificate contains 'LC no.' as a separate field label in the Remarks box (box 5), followed by a specific number '002HMLC253600001'. This is a dedicated LC reference with an explicit bank-term label ('LC no.'), not a substring of a reference number. The LC exception is activated per rule 2.2.

consignor.motivation: LC exception is active per rule 2.2. Consignor verification is not applicable when LC exception is in effect. Only country of origin shall be verified.

rules_applied.description: LC exception active – only country of origin is verified
Identification of origin in invoice – explicit 'Country of Origin' field found
Normalization of country names – direct exact match, no normalization needed

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate box 3 states 'India' as country of origin. Invoice contains an explicit 'Country of Origin' field stating 'India'. These are identical. The origin field in the invoice is a dedicated origin field per rule 2.2 critical rule requirement. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Found – dedicated 'Country of Origin' field on invoice page 1

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'India' (box 3, page 1). Invoice origin: 'India' (explicit 'Country of Origin' field, page 1). Direct exact match. No normalization needed. The invoice field is a dedicated origin field, satisfying the LC exception requirement that origin must be found in an explicit origin field. Confidence 0.99 reflecting exact textual match in a dedicated field.

rule_evaluation_log.outcome: LC exception ACTIVATED. Certificate contains 'LC no. 002HMLC253600001 issued 251226' in box 5 (Remarks). 'LC no.' is a separate field label constituting an explicit bank-term designation per rule 2.2. The number '002HMLC253600001' follows directly. Note: '002HMLC253600001' contains 'LC' as a substring within the number itself, but the activation is based on the LABEL 'LC no.' preceding the number, not the substring. Verification reduced to country of origin only.

Certificate file (Document A) contains pages 1-2 (ORIGINAL and COPY certificate) and page 3 (embedded invoice copy). Invoice file (Document B) contains page 1 (independent commercial invoice). Per rule 8.5.0, verification uses Document B page 1 as the invoice, not the embedded copy on Document A page 3.

PASS. Multiple criteria met: (1) Consignor 'Esswell International AB' found in invoice header and footer; (2) Consignee 'Burayu Development PLC' found in invoice Customer/Applicant; (3) Customer Ref 511202 matches proforma invoice reference.
Invoice contains explicit 'Country of Origin' field stating 'India'. This is a dedicated origin field satisfying both standard verification and LC exception requirements.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet innehåller en LC-hänvisning (Letter of Credit), varför verifieringen begränsas till ursprungsland. Certifikatet anger ursprungsland Indien och fakturan anger uttryckligen 'Country of Origin: India'. Ursprungslandet överensstämmer exakt. Dokumenten kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: LC exception activated. Only country of origin verified. Certificate states 'India', invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: India'. Exact match. All non-applicable checkpoints set to NOT_APPLICABLE per rule 2.2.
non_blocking_notes: Certificate file contains both ORIGINAL and COPY versions – verification based on ORIGINAL (page 1).
Certificate file also contains an embedded invoice copy on page 3 – not used for verification per rule 8.5.0.

Certificate references proforma invoice no. 511202, while commercial invoice number is 833542. The proforma reference (511202) appears as Customer Ref. in the invoice. This is not verified under LC exception.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot faktura

lc_exception.description: Letter of Credit identifierad i certifikatets anmärkningsfält. Verifiering begränsad till ursprungsland enligt regel 2.2.
